body.u: After taking my Os I went on to stay in the sixth form where I took A leavlelevel maths, physics and history I narowlynarrowly scraped passes and by some miracelmiracle gained entrance to a university so far in my life this had been the more prolonged stay away from home but I wasn’t to disturbed at university I tended only to associated with a few people who I had a lot in common with this is how my friendships have been for most of my life but I would blame that on being an only child. ScinceSince a very early age I had been fasenatedfascinated with the past and scincesince the age of fourteen I had regularly atendedattendedarchaeolicalarchaeological digs and so it followed that I got a job as asistentassistant site manager the pay wasn’t much but I was realyreally happy the odd girlfriend came and went but as I spent a great deal of time in a tent or digingdigging holes I didntdidn't get too attached but one day I made the mistake and decided to settle and have a home so I tried to get a post somewhere where I could continue poking in things that have hapendhappened but there were no opening I then I felt I had wasted my education on learning something I liked when I should have lerntlearntsomthingsomething to make money so I had to decided to live as a probably batchelerbachelor doing what I liked or as a someone doing a meanialmenial task but with securetysecurity and a permanatepermanent home on one side was the trap of depesiondepression and povettypoverty and on the other a high chance of not having a family I chose to stay as I was a hoop and if the opertunityopportunity came to have a job I liked where I could still go out and dig but still have a permenatepermanent base I would take it and so when my chance came to take a job in a museammuseum came I leptleapt at it and apart for changing museamsmuseums once or twice I stayed that way till I retired last month. I did get married to someone who I met on a archaeolicalarchaeological site who been in a similar kind of position we had very similar tastes in most things and we both shared nearly the same views of life.
